bids.reasoning: At t=0.0 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting my maximum-utility bid. The opponent opened with all valueD, which is the lowest utility for me on every issue and gives no evidence yet of willingness to make balanced concessions.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still extremely early (t=0.02), and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeating the all-valueD offer that is worst for me. To avoid simply repeating my previous exact bid while still protecting my high-weight issues, I concede only on the lowest-weight issueD.

I am targeting a very high utility bid because it is still early (t=0.04), while making a small concession on my lowest-weight issueD. The opponent’s latest offer improved substantially for me on issueB and issueC but remains very poor on the most important issueA, so I will hold firm on issueA while showing flexibility on issueD.

I am targeting a high-utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.06, but I can concede on the lowest-weight issueD to keep progress moving. The opponent mostly offers valueD across issues, with one brief concession on issueB and issueC, but they reverted afterward, so I will hold firm on the higher-weight issues while testing a low-cost concession.
